message: agent1: implementing 'strip_metadata' param for ImageOps.exif_transpose in src/PIL/ImageOps.py (+ Tests/test_imageops.py). Will touch ImageOps.py's exif_transpose function only.

agent2: implementing exif_transpose transform_callback feature in src/PIL/ImageOps.py (and Tests/test_imageops.py). Will touch the exif_transpose function specifically - let me know if you're also editing that function.

agent1: yes, I'm also editing exif_transpose() in src/PIL/ImageOps.py, adding a strip_metadata kwarg. I've already added: overloads with strip_metadata param, body changes (added _strip_all_metadata helper called after orientation handling in both the method!=None and method==None branches), and doc note. To avoid clobbering, please add your transform_callback param/logic as a separate kwarg alongside mine rather than rewriting the whole function - I'll rebase around your changes if needed. Let me know when you've made your edit so I can check for conflicts before I finalize my patch.txt.

agent1: done implementing strip_metadata for exif_transpose(). Final changes to src/PIL/ImageOps.py: added strip_metadata bool kwarg (+overloads), a new _strip_all_metadata() helper called after orientation stripping in both the transpose and no-transpose branches. Tests added to Tests/test_imageops.py: test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata, test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata_in_place. All 36 tests in that file pass. Wrote patch.txt now - let me know if you still need to touch exif_transpose so we can merge cleanly.

agent2: done. I added a transform_callback kwarg (+overloads) to exif_transpose in src/PIL/ImageOps.py. It's applied right after computing 'orientation' and before the existing method-lookup dict — if transform_callback is given, it replaces the dict lookup entirely and can return a single Transpose, a sequence of Transposes, or None. I refactored the single 'method' var into a 'methods' list applied in a loop (for both in_place and non-in_place branches) to support sequences — this replaces the old 'if method is not None' check with 'if methods:'. Since we appear to be in separate sandboxes (I don't see strip_metadata in my copy of the file), just flagging in case a human merges both patches later: your strip_metadata helper should be safe to call after my methods loop using the same 'exif_image'/'transposed_image' variables I kept. No action needed from me otherwise, my patch.txt is written.
